symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged anterior epistaxis not controlled by first aid

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spontaneous prolonged bleeding failing first aid in an anticoagulated patient signals over-anticoagulation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Long-term warfarin therapy for a mechanical heart valve

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic anticoagulation is the substrate for the coagulopathic bleed and constrains how reversal can be done.
A newly commenced potent warfarin-potentiating antibiotic is the classic precipitant of a supratherapeutic INR bleed.
Negative bleeding screen now, but does not exclude later bleeding on a supratherapeutic INR — remains a mandatory safety-net.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The degree of over-anticoagulation and the need/urgency for reversal cannot be gauged; the AI cannot obtain this remotely, which is precisely why urgent in-person assessment is required.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms iatrogenic over-anticoagulation as the driver — sealed clinician measurement, unavailable via telehealth.
Maintained BP with only mild tachycardia argues against haemodynamic compromise — a clinician-measured value the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active oozing from Little's area, no posterior haemorrhage on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anterior source with no posterior flow makes a posterior bleed less likely — but this can only be confirmed on in-person examination.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the degree of over-anticoagulation and guides reversal; the AI cannot perform it, so it is the concrete reason to arrange urgent in-person assessment.
Assess for anaemia from blood loss during in-person assessment.
